items.headline: US has no option but to drop excessive demands, respect Iran's rights: Deputy FM
items.summary: Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister Saeed Khatibzadeh says the United States has no option but to abandon its excessive demands and fully respect the rights of the Iranian people, adding that diplomacy is the only solution to the crisis.

items.headline: Prospects For New Round Of US-Iran Talks Clouded Two Days Before Truce Expires
items.summary: The prospects for a new round of face-to-face peace talks between the United States and Iran remained unclear amid confusion over the US negotiators' plans and uncertainty over whether Tehran would agree to take part.

items.headline: Iran's military vows 'decisive action' against US army after attack on Iranian vessel
items.summary: Iran's military has vowed to take decisive action against the "terrorist US Army," following an American attack on an Iranian commercial vessel.

items.headline: China voices concern over US attack on Iranian merchant vessel
items.summary: China has voiced its concern over the US attack on an Iranian vessel in the Sea of Oman, warning against any provocations.

items.headline: Oil Jumps, Stocks Ease As U.S.-Iran Ceasefire Hangs In The Balance
items.summary: Oil prices jumped and global equities pulled back as markets grew increasingly concerned that the ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran might not hold, while tensions over the Strait of Hormuz escalated.

items.headline: North Korea showing 'serious ability' over nuclear weapon
items.summary: North Korea is showing a "very serious increase" in its ability to produce nuclear weapons, the head of the International Atomic Energy Agency has said.
